title: Actions and reactions
description: Actions and reactions — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Actions and reactions" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Abdul
memes.description: 'Yellowish boy who serves as Mr. Groombride's interpreter and comically undermines his anti-imperial mission in "Little Foxes."'

memes.label: Aerial Board of Control
memes.description: "The technocratic world government of Kipling's 'With the Night Mail' (2000 A.D.) — the A.B.C. controls all planetary air traffic and, by extension, all governance, having rendered national sovereignty obsolete."

memes.label: America
memes.description: America, the New World nation that serves as a recurring contrast to England throughout Kipling's stories in Actions and Reactions.

memes.label: Atlantic Ocean
memes.description: 'The Atlantic Ocean as traversed by airship in "With the Night Mail" (Chapter 7), a futuristic tale of transatlantic flight set in the year 2000.'

memes.label: Bees and Flies
memes.description: "An allegorical motif in Kipling's 'Actions and Reactions' contrasting the industrious bee with the parasitic fly as a commentary on labour, corruption, and social order."

memes.label: Bulaki Ram
memes.description: The narrator's native clerk who assists with negotiations during the cotton deal and knows English Law in Chapter 9, "A Deal in Cotton."

memes.label: Burnt House
memes.description: 'One of the five satellite farms of Friars Pardon, situated under High Pardons Wood, in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Captain Hodgson
memes.description: 'Relief captain of the G.P.O. airship "162" in "With the Night Mail," a large red aeronaut who serves as technical guide and philosophical commentator.'

memes.label: Captain Purnall
memes.description: 'Captain of the airship "162" in "With the Night Mail," a skilled pilot navigating the transatlantic Aerial Board of Control routes.'

memes.label: Centro-Euro-Africa Protectorate
memes.description: 'A fictional African colonial protectorate where Adam Strickland serves as Assistant-Commissioner in "A Deal in Cotton".'

memes.label: "Chapter 1: An Habitation Enforced"
memes.description: "My friend, if cause doth wrest thee, Ere folly hath much oppressed thee, Far from acquaintance kest thee Where country…"

memes.label: "Chapter 2: The Recall"
memes.description: "I am the land of their fathers, In me the virtue stays; I will bring back my children, After certain days."

memes.label: "Chapter 3: Garm — a Hostage"
memes.description: "One night, a very long time ago, I drove to an Indian military cantonment called Mian Mir to see amateur theatricals."

memes.label: "Chapter 4: The Power of the Dog"
memes.description: "There is sorrow enough in the natural way From men and women to fill our day; But when we are certain of sorrow in…"

memes.label: "Chapter 5: The Mother Hive"
memes.description: "If the stock had not been old and overcrowded, the Wax-moth would never have entered; but where bees are too thick on…"

memes.label: "Chapter 6: The Bees and the Flies"
memes.description: "A farmer of the Augustan age Perused in Virgil's golden page, The story of the secret won From Proteus by Cyrene's son…"

memes.label: "Chapter 7: With the Night Mail"
memes.description: 'A STORY OF 2000 A. D. (Together with extracts from the magazine in which it appeared) A nine o''clock of a gusty…'

memes.label: "Chapter 8: The Four Angels"
memes.description: "As ADAM lay a-dreaming beneath the Apple Tree, The Angel of the Earth came down, and offered Earth in fee."

memes.label: "Chapter 9: A Deal in Cotton"
memes.description: "Long and long ago, when Devadatta was King of Benares, I wrote some tales concerning Strickland of the Punjab Police…"

memes.label: "Chapter 10: The New Knighthood"
memes.description: "Who gives him the Bath? “I,” said the wet, Rank Jungle-sweat, “I'll give him the Bath!” Who'll sing the psalms?"

memes.label: "Chapter 11: The Puzzler"
memes.description: "I had not seen Penfentenyou since the Middle Nineties, when he was Minister of Ways and Woodsides in De Thouar's first…"

memes.label: "Chapter 12: The Puzzler (poem)"
memes.description: "The Celt in all his variants from Builth to Ballyhoo, His mental processes are plain--one knows what he will do, And…"

memes.label: "Chapter 13: Little Foxes"
memes.description: "A TALE OF THE GIHON HUNT A fox came out of his earth on the banks of the Great River Gihon, which waters Ethiopia."

memes.label: "Chapter 14: Gallio's Song"
memes.description: "All day long to the judgment-seat The crazed Provincials drew-- All day long at their ruler's feet Howled for the blood…"

memes.label: "Chapter 15: The House Surgeon"
memes.description: "On an evening after Easter Day, I sat at a table in a homeward bound steamer's smoking-room, where half a dozen of us…"

memes.label: "Chapter 16: The Rabbi's Song"
memes.description: "If thought can reach to Heaven, On Heaven let it dwell, For fear that Thought be given Like power to reach to Hell."

memes.label: Cotton
memes.description: "Cotton as an agricultural commodity, economic venture, and symbol of colonial enterprise in Kipling's ch09 A Deal in Cotton."

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: 'Courage as a professional virtue in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the calm competence of airmen facing danger in "With the Night Mail" and the quieter courage of characters confronting change.'

memes.label: A Deal in Cotton
memes.description: 'A frame story in which Adam Strickland recounts a cotton negotiation in Africa, told during a reunion of Anglo-Indian friends in England.'

memes.label: Depression
memes.description: 'Depression as psychological affliction in Kipling''s "The House Surgeon" — an oppressive mental state that haunts a house and its inhabitants.'

memes.label: Dirigible
memes.description: "Dirigible balloons as the primary mode of aerial mail transport in Kipling's futuristic ch07 With the Night Mail, set in the year 2000 AD."

memes.label: Dr. Dallas
memes.description: "The local doctor near Friars Pardon, summoned by Mrs. Betts to attend the dying Iggulden and later consulted about the ailing Miss Claxton in Chapter 1."

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: 'A central moral theme in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the sense of obligation that drives characters across social classes, professions, and imperial settings.'

memes.label: Earth
memes.description: 'The earth as a grounding force in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the literal soil of England that roots identity, the planet transformed by aerial technology, and the contested ground between wild and cultivated.'

memes.label: England
memes.description: "The primary setting of Kipling's 'Actions and Reactions' — an England of country estates, tenant farms, and ancient obligations that confronts the American Chapins with the weight of place."

memes.label: Ethiopia
memes.description: 'Setting of "Little Foxes" (Chapter 13), where the Gihon River flows and British colonial administration contends with reformist meddling.'

memes.label: Fever
memes.description: "Recurring motif of fever as physical illness and narrative catalyst across Kipling's Actions and Reactions, most prominently in ch03 Garm and ch09 A Deal in Cotton."

memes.label: Four Angels
memes.description: 'The four angelic figures from Chapter 8 of Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — a theological allegory exploring divine justice, intercession, and the limits of heavenly intervention in human affairs.'

memes.label: Friars Pardon
memes.description: 'Country estate in the Sussex Weald, ancestral home restored by George and Sophie Chapin in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Gale Anstey
memes.description: 'One of the five satellite farms of the Friars Pardon estate, home to old Whybarne, in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: "Gallio's Song"
memes.description: "Kipling's poetic dramatisation of Gallio the Roman proconsul's refusal to adjudicate religious disputes, from ch14 of Actions and Reactions."
aliases: I care for none of these things

memes.label: Garden
memes.description: 'The garden as a central image in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — England conceived as a cultivated garden, the terraced gardens of Friars Pardon, and the garden as a site of both order and loss.'

memes.label: Garm
memes.description: 'A prize bull-terrier given as a hostage-gift by a soldier to the narrator in "Garm — a Hostage", noted for his intelligence and loyalty.'

memes.label: Garm Given as Hostage
memes.description: 'A soldier gives his prize bull-terrier Garm to the narrator as repayment for being rescued from military discipline in "Garm — a Hostage".'

memes.label: Gentleman
memes.description: 'The ideal of the gentleman in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — a social identity defined by conduct rather than birth, operating across English, Brazilian, and Mohammedan cultural contexts.'

memes.label: George Chapin
memes.description: 'American financier who retreats to rural England after a nervous breakdown, protagonist of "An Habitation Enforced".'

memes.label: Ghost
memes.description: 'The ghost in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from the literal ghost stories of "The House Surgeon" to the metaphorical ghosts of landscape, memory, and conscience that haunt the collection.'

memes.label: Gihon
memes.description: 'The Gihon, a great river in Africa, setting of the Gihon Hunt in "Little Foxes" (Chapter 13).'

memes.label: Giuseppe
memes.description: 'Italian organ-grinder and servant at Lord Lundie's estate, whose barrel-organ becomes the instrument for luring a monkey in "The Puzzler."'

memes.label: Imperial Government
memes.description: "The diffuse apparatus of British imperial governance that appears throughout Kipling's 'Actions and Reactions' as both an administrative reality and a target of satirical scrutiny."

memes.label: Holmescroft
memes.description: 'Holmescroft, a large two-storied creeper-covered residence in England, home of the M''Leod family, central setting of "The House Surgeon" (Chapter 15).'

memes.label: Holz and Gunsberg Combine
memes.description: 'A financial combine targeted by George Chapin before his nervous breakdown in "An Habitation Enforced", representing American corporate power.'

memes.label: Home
memes.description: 'Home as the gravitational centre of belonging in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the ancestral English house that claims the Chapins, the cottage to which Garm must return, and the aerial beacon that guides the night mail back.'

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: 'A social and moral code governing reputation, truthfulness, and standing in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions."'

memes.label: House Surgeon
memes.description: "The metaphorical role of one who diagnoses and heals the psychological or spiritual ailments of a house, as dramatised in Kipling's ch15 The House Surgeon."

memes.label: Ibn Makarrah
memes.description: A hostile tribal leader who waged war against the British before annexation and whose men continue to threaten the region in Chapter 9, "A Deal in Cotton."

memes.label: Iggulden
memes.description: "A tenant farmer family at Rocketts Farm in 'An Habitation Enforced' — old Iggulden represents the dying generation of English rural labour, while his son inherits the land and its duties."

memes.label: Imam Din
memes.description: 'Muslim servant to Strickland in "A Deal in Cotton," who assists in the cotton speculation scheme and cares for the fever-stricken narrator.'

memes.label: India
memes.description: India, the British colonial territory that serves as the backdrop for many characters and events across Kipling's Actions and Reactions stories.

memes.label: Jimmy
memes.description: "An artist and old Harrovian who serves as interpreter during the monkey-puzzler experiment — known for his high voice, linguistic facility, and the Artistic Temperament in 'The Puzzler'."

memes.label: John Baxter
memes.description: A large, throaty-voiced solicitor who handled the sale of Holmescroft and becomes a key figure in uncovering the house's mysterious affliction in Chapter 15.

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: 'Justice as a moral expectation in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from the ancient court of hounds in "Little Foxes" to the personal plea for fair treatment in "The Mother Hive."'

memes.label: Kadir Buksh
memes.description: The narrator's trusted native attendant who assists with Garm and provides quiet, capable service during the events of Chapter 3, "Garm — a Hostage."

memes.label: Lady Conant
memes.description: 'Local aristocrat in "An Habitation Enforced" who takes an interest in Sophie Chapin and the future of Friars Pardon.'

memes.label: Law
memes.description: 'Law as a social ordering principle in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from English property law to Mosaic dietary law, from colonial legal authority to the futuristic "Lavalle''s Law" of atmospheric physics.'

memes.label: Little Foxes
memes.description: 'An allegorical concept from Chapter 13 of Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the "little foxes" that spoil the vines, representing petty corruptions that undermine colonial governance.'

memes.label: London
memes.description: "The metropolitan hub of Kipling's Edwardian world — a place of solicitors, business, and imperial administration that serves as the counterpoint to rural England throughout 'Actions and Reactions'."

memes.label: Lord Lundie
memes.description: "A Law Lord and the final authority on legal and constitutional questions in 'The Puzzler' — a figure of wry judicial wisdom who hosts Penfentenyou at his Credence Green farm during the colonial premier's English visit."

memes.label: Love
memes.description: 'Love as a binding and testing force in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the love of land that transforms the Chapins, the love between man and dog that defies ownership, and the love of work that defines the hive.'

memes.label: Madness
memes.description: 'Madness as divine affliction and psychological motif in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from God-sent disease to the brain''s reflected chaos.'

memes.label: Mary Cloke
memes.description: 'The daughter of Mr. and Mrs. Cloke, a sewing maid at Pardons who assists Sophie Chapin in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Melissa
memes.description: A young, practical-minded worker bee who first encounters the Wax-moth infiltrator and helps lead the loyal swarm to safety in Chapter 5, "The Mother Hive."

memes.label: Mian Mir
memes.description: "An Indian military cantonment near Lahore, setting for the opening of \"Garm — a Hostage\" and site of the narrator's rescue of a soldier friend."

memes.label: Agnes Moultrie
memes.description: "The youngest Moultrie sister, whose death at Holmescroft — long believed a suicide — is ultimately revealed as an accident caused by the house's depressive affliction in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Miss Elizabeth Moultrie
memes.description: "The middle Moultrie sister, nicknamed Bessie, who is timid and easily influenced, serving as a foil to her imperious elder sister Mary in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Miss Elizabeth Moultrie
memes.description: "The youngest surviving Moultrie sister, physically small and emotionally vulnerable, who wrestles with her elder sister and the house's affliction in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Miss Mary Moultrie
memes.description: "The eldest of the three Moultrie sisters, haunted by the depressive affliction of Holmescroft, who seeks the truth about her youngest sister Agnes's death in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Miss M'Leod
memes.description: "The daughter of the M'Leod family, called Thea, whose youthful vitality is the first casualty of Holmescroft's depressive influence in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Money
memes.description: 'Money as a medium of exchange and power in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the purchase of Friars Pardon, the cotton speculation in Chapter 9, and the tension between inherited wealth and commercial acquisition.'

memes.label: Mother Hive
memes.description: 'Central allegorical concept in "The Mother Hive" — the beehive as a metaphor for British society facing internal decay and ideological subversion.'

memes.label: Mr. Cloke
memes.description: 'The farmer at Rocketts who, with his wife Mrs. Cloke, hosts George and Sophie Chapin upon their arrival in the English countryside in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Mr. L.L. Geary
memes.description: 'Second Despatcher and learned official aboard the G.P.O. night mail airship in "With the Night Mail."'

memes.label: Mr. Lethabie Groombride
memes.description: "A self-righteous British MP who travels to Ethiopia to investigate alleged colonial abuses, only to be spectacularly outmaneuvered by the local administration in Chapter 13."

memes.label: L. Maxwell M'Leod
memes.description: "A City man and owner of Holmescroft, who discovers too late that his country house harbors a sinister depressive affliction that threatens his entire family in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Mrs. Betts
memes.description: "A small, capable villager near Friars Pardon who responds to Iggulden's collapse with characteristic English phlegm — 'They come down like ellum-branches in still weather' — in Chapter 1."

memes.label: Mrs. Cloke
memes.description: 'The farmer wife at Rocketts who hosts the Chapins during their countryside retreat in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Mrs. M'Leod
memes.description: "The wife of L. Maxwell M'Leod, whose suffering from Holmescroft's depressive affliction reveals the house's insidious power over the family in Chapter 15."

memes.label: Mrs. Shonts
memes.description: 'American expatriate who recommends the English countryside to Sophie Chapin in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Music
memes.description: 'Music as a recurring artistic motif in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from the songs and poems embedded in the collection to the music-room as a site of social performance and emotional release.'

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: 'Nature as the given order of persons and things in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from the "nature of things" to "his or her nature" as character destiny.'

memes.label: Night Mail Flight
memes.description: "A transatlantic flight aboard Postal Packet 162 in Kipling's futuristic 'With the Night Mail' (set in 2000 A.D.) — a journey through the technological sublime of a world governed by the Aerial Board of Control."

memes.label: Order
memes.description: 'Order as command, structure, and social foundation in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from military discipline to the putting-right of houses and worlds.'

memes.label: Penfentenyou
memes.description: "Colonial Premier and Minister of Ways and Woodsides in De Thouar's first Administration — an old Harrovian who travels to England to develop a Great Idea for his growing country in 'The Puzzler'."

memes.label: Power of the Dog
memes.description: "A recurring motif in Kipling's 'Actions and Reactions' exploring the bond between humans and dogs as a metaphor for loyalty, power, and moral obligation."

memes.label: Punjab Police
memes.description: 'The police force of the Punjab province in British India, where Strickland served before retiring to England.'

memes.label: "Rabbi's Song"
memes.description: 'The Rabbi''s Song — Kipling''s closing poem in "Actions and Reactions," a meditation on divine foreknowledge, human madness, and the consolation of faith.'

memes.label: Rocketts Farm
memes.description: 'A farm in the southern English counties where George and Sophie Chapin recuperate and rediscover ancestral English life in "An Habitation Enforced".'

memes.label: Sacharissa
memes.description: A disguised Wax-moth who infiltrates the Mother Hive in Chapter 5, laying eggs that threaten to destroy the colony from within.

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: 'Sacrifice as a moral and narrative motif in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from ancestral land-devotion to personal cost and social obligation.'

memes.label: Sangres
memes.description: A Brazilian gentleman who purchased four farms in England, becoming a figure of suspicion and envy among the local community in Chapter 1.

memes.label: Servant
memes.description: 'The servant as a social institution in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — domestic service, colonial body-servants, and the master-servant relationship as a microcosm of imperial hierarchy.'

memes.label: Sheshaheli
memes.description: 'The Sheshaheli country, a tribal region in Africa central to the cotton dealings in "A Deal in Cotton" (Chapter 9).'

memes.label: Sir Christopher Tomling
memes.description: 'A political figure and friend of Lord Lundie who contributes a puzzle about the Empire to the gathering at Credence Green in "The Puzzler."'

memes.label: Sir Walter Conant
memes.description: 'Baronet and large landholder in the neighbourhood of Friars Pardon, husband of Lady Conant in "An Habitation Enforced."'

memes.label: Sophie Chapin
memes.description: 'Wife of George Chapin, who accompanies him through his nervous breakdown recovery and rural English retreat in "An Habitation Enforced".'

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: 'The soul as the irreducible self in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the alien soul seeking anchorage, the house that possesses a soul, and the bruised soul that must speak truth.'

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: 'The dual meaning of spirit in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — the psychological attitude of "proper spirit" and the supernatural presence that haunts the house in "The House Surgeon."'

memes.label: Stalky
memes.description: 'Colonel A.L. Corkran, nicknamed Stalky, a veteran commander of native Infantry on the Indian frontier and friend in "A Deal in Cotton".'

memes.label: Stanley
memes.description: A soldier stationed at Kasauli, original owner of the dog Garm, whose good conduct is secured by the narrator holding Garm as a hostage.

memes.label: Strickland
memes.description: 'Veteran officer of the Punjab Police who appears in multiple Kipling stories, now retired to Weston-super-Mare.'

memes.label: The Hajji
memes.description: A revered elder and Hajji (pilgrim to Mecca) in the cotton region, who serves as guide and mediator in the deal of cotton narrated in Chapter 9.

memes.label: The Puzzler
memes.description: "A diplomatic comedy in Kipling's 'Actions and Reactions' — the Colonial Premier Penfentenyou visits England to advance a Great Idea, only to be chased by his own Agent-General in a motor car through a maze of imperial politics."

memes.label: The Recall
memes.description: 'A central theme in "Actions and Reactions" — the magnetic pull of England drawing its scattered children home to ancestral land and identity.'

memes.label: Tradition
memes.description: 'The inherited framework of custom and expectation that structures social life in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from land tenure to fox-hunting ritual.'

memes.label: Vixen
memes.description: The narrator's fox-terrier, fiercely loyal and jealous, whose interactions with Garm the bull-terrier form a key subplot in Chapter 3.

memes.label: War
memes.description: 'War as a background presence and commercial reality in Kipling''s "Actions and Reactions" — from colonial skirmishes to the futurist abolition of war in the Aerial Board era.'

memes.label: Wax-moth
memes.description: 'Allegorical parasite in "The Mother Hive" representing insidious ideological corruption that exploits weakness and division within communities.'

memes.label: The Wax-moth Invasion of the Hive
memes.description: 'A wax-moth enters an overheated, overcrowded beehive and systematically subverts it from within in "The Mother Hive".'

memes.label: Weston-super-Mare
memes.description: 'English seaside town where Strickland of the Punjab Police retires after completing his Indian Service, mentioned in "A Deal in Cotton".'

memes.label: Whybarne
memes.description: 'Elderly resident of Gale Anstey and local sage in "An Habitation Enforced," who has seen seventy-eight years and embodies the deep-rooted rural perspective.'
